meta.title: Performance and Caching
meta.excerpt: Core Web Vitals optimization, caching strategies, image optimization, and bundle analysis for the Next.js application.

seo.metaDescription: Core Web Vitals optimization and caching strategies

props.text: Comprehensive performance optimization documentation covering Core Web Vitals targets, caching strategies, image optimization, code splitting, and bundle analysis.

links.title: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
links.description:
Target: < 2.5 seconds

Measures when the largest visible element loads.

props.body:
Optimize server response time (TTFB < 600ms)
• Inline critical CSS
• Preload largest images
• Defer non-critical JavaScript
• Use CDN for static assets

links.title: First Input Delay (FID)
links.description:
Target: < 100 milliseconds

Measures responsiveness to user input.

props.body:
Reduce JavaScript execution time
• Break up long tasks into smaller chunks
• Use useTransition for non-blocking updates
• Optimize main thread work
• Progressive enhancement for slow networks

links.title: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)
links.description:
Target: < 0.1

Measures visual stability during loading.

props.body:
Reserve space for dynamic content
• Avoid inserting content above existing content
• Use CSS transforms for animations
• Specify image dimensions
• Use font-display: swap for web fonts

links.title: Next.js Built-in Caching
links.description: Use Data Cache, Request Deduplication, Request Memoization, Router Cache, and Full Route Cache to reduce load time and API pressure.

props.body:
Data Cache for static and revalidated fetches
• Request Deduplication for repeated server requests
• Request Memoization during a single render pass
• Router Cache for navigation speed
• Full Route Cache for static routes

Use Next Image for responsive delivery and automatic optimization
• Always specify width and height to prevent layout shift
• Use priority for above-the-fold images
• Compress images before upload
• Lazy load below-the-fold images

props.title: Dynamic Import Pattern

props.body:
Split code into separate bundles
• Load heavy features only when needed
• Show skeletons during async component loading
• Use route-based splitting from Next.js app router

Use @next/bundle-analyzer to inspect bundle composition
• Track Lighthouse metrics in CI
• Set performance budgets and alert on regressions
• Keep main bundle under 50KB gzipped when possible

Collect Web Vitals from real users
• Track business-event latency metrics
• Integrate error tracking and performance traces
• Run regular PageSpeed and Lighthouse audits
